content.title: My dad wants to buy me a big suburban home — but I don’t want to live there. Am I ungrateful if I turn him down?
content.summary: “I would rather have something small, on a huge piece of land in a more rural area.”

content.title: Iran war hasn’t hurt the U.S. economy too badly — but the danger isn’t over yet
content.summary: The two main pillars of the economy — consumer spending and business investment — appear to have shrugged off early angst arising from Iran tensions.

content.title: Taylor Farms to recall produce that may be the cause of the cyclospora outbreak: report
content.summary: The California-based company is one of the largest producers of salad greens in the U.S.

content.title: ‘I am at a loss’: My brother is dying. We are not close. What responsibility do I have to help him?
content.summary: “He is now no longer able to think clearly or communicate his wishes.”

content.title: Why Trump’s speech on U.S. elections could be bad for markets
content.summary: The president focused in part on accusing China of “sinister election meddling” in 2020.

content.title: SpaceX postponed a crucial launch — and now its stock is sliding further below the IPO price
content.summary: Shares in SpaceX declined about 4% in premarket trading on Friday after aborted mission.

content.title: I’m 67 with a $140,000 pension. Should I wait until 70 to claim Social Security so my wife gets more?
content.summary: “When I pass, all my retirement income is reduced to $30,000 a year.”

content.title: Apple’s stock is beating the S&P 500 by a remarkable degree — and it may have more room to run
content.summary: Apple was once seen as behind the curve in AI, but now it’s getting more respect for its strategic choices.

content.title: ‘They are relentless’: A scammer is trying to change the email on my empty Robinhood account. What do they have to gain?
content.summary: “I’ve just been ignoring their attempts.”

content.title: 18 tech stocks that have fallen at least 30% during July
content.summary: Seven of the worst performers for the month were still up by triple digits for 2026.
